data.title: Fed Chair Powell to Speak at Harvard Amidst Market Focus on Labor Data
data.description: According to Jin10, the U.S. market is set for an eventful week as it transitions from March to April, with labor market data returning to the spotlight. However, the most anticipated event on the agenda is the appearance of Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell later today at 22:30. Powell is scheduled to participate in a moderated discussion as part of Harvard University's introductory economics course. While the session lacks a specific theme, it is expected that Powell will address a wide range of topics, including the economy, inflation, and monetary policy. Analysts predict that he will steer clear of politically sensitive issues related to the Federal Reserve. Nonetheless, it is anticipated that U.S. President Donald Trump may criticize Powell following the event, particularly if there are indications that the Fed intends to pause rate adjustments amidst the Middle Eastern situation to maintain flexibility. Ironically, this situation has been influenced by Trump's own actions, yet he is likely to blame Powell for not lowering rates.

data.title: STOCKS | CMB International Securities Lowers Pop Mart Target Price by 66.6%
data.description: CMB International Securities has significantly reduced its target price for Pop Mart (09992.HK) by 66.6%, from HKD 380 to HKD 127, and downgraded its rating from 'Overweight' to 'Underweight.' According to Jin10, the firm has also lowered its overseas revenue forecasts for Pop Mart by 52% for 2026 and 60% for 2027, anticipating that single-store sales in the U.S. and Thailand will be 20% and 30% lower than previously expected, respectively. Due to the decline in overseas sales proportion, the gross margin forecasts for 2026 and 2027 have been adjusted down by 6 and 10 percentage points, respectively. Overall, the net profit forecasts for 2026 and 2027 have been reduced by 32% and 43%. The firm noted that earnings in the second half of 2025 are expected to be lower than high expectations, with growth in the Americas slowing down. The conservative guidance reflects structural adjustments rather than profit fluctuations.

data.title: Toyota Faces Sales Decline Amidst EV Competition and Domestic Challenges
data.description: Toyota experienced a slight decline in sales in February, primarily due to intense competition in the electric vehicle (EV) market in China and reduced demand in Japan. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the sales figures do not yet account for the effects of the Iran conflict, which began on the final day of the month.

data.title: Israel Approves 2026 Budget with Increased Defense Spending
data.description: Israel's parliament has approved the 2026 state budget, which includes a significant increase in defense spending to address the ongoing conflict with Iran. Bloomberg posted on X that the budget will be financed through additional borrowing and reductions in civilian expenditure. The decision reflects the government's prioritization of national security amid heightened tensions in the region. The increased defense allocation aims to bolster Israel's military capabilities and readiness in response to perceived threats from Iran. This budgetary move underscores the government's commitment to ensuring the country's security while managing fiscal challenges.

data.title: Jefferies Maintains 'Buy' Rating on Meituan Amid Improved Business Fundamentals
data.description: According to Jin10, Jefferies released a research report indicating that Meituan's total revenue for the fourth quarter of last year increased by 4.1% year-on-year to 92.1 billion RMB, aligning with both the firm's and market expectations. The core local business revenue saw a 1% year-on-year decline to 64.8 billion RMB, slightly below the market's expectation of remaining flat, while Jefferies anticipated a 1.3% drop. The core local business operating loss reached 10 billion RMB. New business revenue grew by 18.9% year-on-year to 27.3 billion RMB, surpassing expectations, with an operating loss of 4.6 billion RMB. The adjusted net loss amounted to 15 billion RMB, which was below market expectations but in line with Jefferies' forecast. Jefferies noted that Meituan's core local business fundamentals are improving, and new business ventures are on track, maintaining a 'buy' rating with a target price of 130 HKD.

data.title: Strategy has increased its holdings by approximately 45,000 BTC in the past 30 days, representing about 76% of the demand from reserve-type companies.
data.description: According to a CryptoQuant report, Strategy has accumulated approximately 45,000 BTC in the past 30 days, marking its fastest accumulation rate in nearly a year. Its current purchases account for about 76% of the demand from all reserve-holding companies. The report also points out that purchases by companies other than Strategy have decreased significantly, totaling only about 1,000 BTC in the past 30 days, representing about 2% of the total. The number of purchases has also decreased from a peak of 54 to 13, indicating that new demand in the market is becoming more concentrated.

data.title: Circle Shares Decline Amid Regulatory Concerns and Tether's Audit Announcement
data.description: Circle's shares concluded last week at $93.6, marking a decline of approximately 26% from their March 24 opening near $126. According to NS3.AI, this drop follows the introduction of a Senate draft bill that suggests a potential ban on passive stablecoin yields. In related developments, Tether announced it has engaged a major accounting firm to conduct its first audit of reserves. Analysts attribute the selloff to growing uncertainty surrounding Circle's business model.

data.title: AI TRENDS | London's Tech Scene Gains Momentum with AI Innovations
data.description: London is experiencing a significant surge in its tech industry, driven by advancements in artificial intelligence from major players like Google DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic, alongside emerging local startups. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the city's potential to become a leading tech hub. This growth is attributed to the innovative developments and investments in AI technologies, which are positioning London as a key player in the global tech landscape. The city's tech ecosystem is benefiting from a combination of established companies and new entrants, fostering a dynamic environment for technological advancements. As these AI-driven initiatives continue to evolve, London's reputation as a tech powerhouse is expected to strengthen, attracting further investment and talent to the region.

data.title: Nomura Securities Revises Fed Rate Cut Forecast to September and December
data.description: Nomura Securities has updated its forecast for the U.S. Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts, now predicting reductions of 25 basis points each in September and December. According to Jin10, this revision contrasts with their earlier projection, which anticipated rate cuts in June and September. The adjustment reflects Nomura's reassessment of the economic landscape and the Fed's potential policy trajectory.

data.title: Oil Supply Shortfall Amid Iran Conflict Sparks Demand Destruction Concerns
data.description: The ongoing conflict in Iran has led to significant disruptions in oil supplies, raising concerns about how to address the resulting shortfall. Bloomberg posted on X that the only viable solution to this issue, should the conflict persist, is demand destruction. This approach involves reducing consumption to balance the supply-demand equation in the global oil market. The situation underscores the vulnerability of energy markets to geopolitical tensions and the potential economic impacts of prolonged supply disruptions. As the conflict continues, stakeholders are closely monitoring developments and considering strategies to mitigate the effects on global oil supplies.

data.title: Ethereum's Bear Flag Pattern Signals Potential Downside
data.description: Ethereum has dropped below a crucial technical support level, displaying a bear flag pattern that could indicate further declines. According to NS3.AI, Ethereum ETF flows have been negative since last September, and the slower minting of USDC along with net outflows suggests that ecosystem liquidity remains fragile.

data.title: BIT: ETH breaks below key support level; bearish flag pattern may continue the downtrend.
data.description: BIT, in a post on the X platform, stated that Ethereum has broken below a key technical support level and is currently exhibiting a bearish flag pattern, which typically indicates a potential continuation of the downtrend. They pointed out that a similar structure appeared in January of this year, followed by a significant price decline. Regarding liquidity, they noted persistently low trading volume, insufficient market participation, and a continuous net outflow from the Ethereum ETF since September of last year, with no recent significant improvement. Simultaneously, USDC minting has slowed and turned into a net outflow, reflecting the still unstable recovery of liquidity within the ecosystem. Given the lack of a significant rebound in on-chain activity and DeFi demand, Ethereum's demand recovery will still take time.

data.title: Prediction Market Transactions Surge in March
data.description: Prediction market transactions reached an unprecedented 191 million in March. According to NS3.AI, the monthly notional trading volume also saw a significant increase, climbing to $23.9 billion. Data from Dune indicates that transactions surged by 2,838% compared to the same period last year.

data.title: Singapore's Monetary Policy May Remain Unchanged Amid Short-Term US-Iran Conflict, Analysts Say
data.description: On March 30, analysts from Fitch's BMI unit indicated that the Monetary Authority of Singapore's policy response could keep both overall and core inflation rates at an average of 1.8% in 2026. According to Jin10, the analysts expect that if the US-Iran conflict is brief, the Monetary Authority of Singapore will maintain its policy settings unchanged in 2026. However, they believe that if the conflict extends beyond March, the risk of policy tightening in April will increase. Should the Monetary Authority of Singapore tighten its policy, the Singapore dollar exchange rate could be stronger than BMI's current forecast of 1 USD to 1.28 SGD by the end of 2026.

data.title: S&P 500 Faces Potential Decline Amid Rising Oil Prices
data.description: The S&P 500 index may experience further declines as oil prices continue to rise. According to ChainCatcher, Paul Chew from Phillip Securities Research highlighted that since the outbreak of the Middle East conflict, the index has already fallen by 7.9% from its peak. Chew noted that during the Gulf War and the Russia-Ukraine conflict, the index saw declines of 17%. He emphasized that the market still has room for further downward movement, particularly with the ongoing increase in oil prices.

data.title: New Zealand Finance Minister Willis: Treasury Yet to Update Inflation Forecasts
data.description: New Zealand's Finance Minister, Willis, stated that the Treasury has not yet provided an update on inflation forecasts. According to Jin10, the Treasury maintains a range of inflation expectation scenarios.

data.title: Binance to Suspend Moonriver Network Transactions for Upgrade
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, starting at approximately 2026-03-30 12:00 (UTC), the platform will temporarily suspend deposits and withdrawals of tokens on the Moonriver (MOVR) network. This suspension is to facilitate a network upgrade aimed at enhancing user experience. The upgrade is scheduled to occur at block height 15,692,116, which is expected to be reached around 2026-03-30 13:00 (UTC).

The trading of tokens on the Moonriver network will remain unaffected during this period. Binance assures users that it will manage all technical requirements associated with the upgrade. Once the network is deemed stable post-upgrade, deposits and withdrawals will be reopened. However, no further announcements will be made regarding the reopening. Users are advised to stay informed through the project team's communications for any additional updates.

data.title: Musk Announces Automatic Translation Feature for Grok
data.description: Elon Musk has announced that Grok has begun automatically translating and recommending posts in other languages. According to Odaily, this new feature identifies foreign language content and translates it directly into users' feeds, eliminating the need for manual translation. Users have reported seeing a significant number of translated foreign language posts in their timelines.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Chinese Gold Jewelry Prices Remain Steady
data.description: According to Jin10, on March 30, the prices of gold jewelry in China are generally stable, ranging between 1,385 yuan and 1,395 yuan per gram. Notably, the prices for Chow Tai Seng's 999 pure gold, Laopu Gold's pure gold jewelry, and Chow Tai Fook's pure gold items, including jewelry and ornaments, are all set at 1,395 yuan per gram.

data.title: Global Sovereign Bonds Rise Amid Middle East Conflict Concerns
data.description:
Global sovereign bonds are experiencing an upswing due to fears that the Middle East conflict could hinder global economic growth. According to Jin10, this has reignited market interest in government bonds that were previously sold off. During the Asian trading session, U.S., Australian, and Japanese government bonds saw gains, as the market speculates that soaring oil prices may signal a long-term global energy shortage. This has bolstered demand for government bonds, which had faced selling pressure recently due to inflation concerns overshadowing their traditional safe-haven appeal.

Macquarie Group strategist Gareth Berry noted, "If the Middle East war remains unresolved, the market is now boldly imagining what the world will look like in a month. People are already drawing parallels to the COVID-19 pandemic period, as the economy faces shutdown risks—this time due to a lack of fuel." Prior to this bond rally, the market had endured weeks of sell-offs due to rising oil prices and concerns over potential central bank interest rate hikes. Recently, the market's focus has shifted to slowing economic growth, alleviating fears that central banks might need to adopt aggressive hawkish stances to control inflation.
